Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-16 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
Today's data reveals two exceptional long-term performers at the top of the momentum ranker. CLMT (rank #1, score 95.2) shows explosive near-term gains with a 1-year return of 152% and 5-year return of 535%, though it significantly underperformed over 10 years (down 114% vs SPY). LINC ranks second with even more dramatic long-term results, posting a stunning 1,206% 10-year return that vastly outpaces SPY by 973%, yet its momentum ratios are more modest than CLMT's, suggesting steadier rather than accelerating growth. Both stocks pass all moving average tests and show "strong_buy" signals, but CLMT's concerning "bad_spy_score" of 1.32 indicates potential concentration risk or sector-specific volatility, whereas LINC's score of 0.21 suggests more balanced risk characteristics. The key divergence is CLMT's recent acceleration (153% in 1 year after 535% in 5 years) versus LINC's steadier long-term trajectory, presenting different risk profiles for longer-horizon investors.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
